part_title: Section Twenty-Two: Ready-to-Use Field Records and Forms
part_subtitle: Field Records, Monitoring Forms and Operational Templates

items.text: A scientific programme for managing Tuta absoluta cannot be implemented without a clear recording system because sound decisions require knowledge of what actually happened in the field, not what the team remembers several days or weeks later.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A scientific programme for managing Tuta absoluta cannot be implemented without a clear recording system because sound decisions require knowledge of what actually happened in the field, not what the team remembers several days or weeks later.

items.text: Records make it possible to determine:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Records make it possible to determine:

items: What percentage of eggs was parasitised?
Did new infestation appear after treatment?
Did an immigration wave occur from a neighbouring field?
What was the programme's effect on fruit and yield?
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Did an immigration wave occur from a neighbouring field?
[[prepared]]What was the programme's effect on fruit and yield?

items.text: Records are not an administrative activity separate from pest management. They are part of the system for decision-making, resistance management, residue compliance, and performance analysis.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Records are not an administrative activity separate from pest management. They are part of the system for decision-making, resistance management, residue compliance, and performance analysis.
[[prepared]]The recording system aims to:

items: 2. Reduce dependence on memory and subjective impressions.
3. Compare field edges with the field interior.
5. Link pest status with weather and crop stage.
6. Document the reasons for intervention and non-intervention.
12. Calculate fruit infestation and accepted yield.
14. Develop local thresholds in the future.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]2. Reduce dependence on memory and subjective impressions.
[[prepared]]3. Compare field edges with the field interior.
[[prepared]]5. Link pest status with weather and crop stage.
[[prepared]]6. Document the reasons for intervention and non-intervention.
[[prepared]]12. Calculate fruit infestation and accepted yield.
[[prepared]]14. Develop local thresholds in the future.

items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A record should be:

items: Linked to a known field and sector.
Signed or attributable to a specific inspector.
Easy to convert into a database.

[[prepared]]Signed or attributable to a specific inspector.

items.text: The field identification card is the primary reference for all records and should be completed before planting or when monitoring begins.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The field identification card is the primary reference for all records and should be completed before planting or when monitoring begins.

items.text: Record of Adjacent Fields and Risk Sources
This form records surrounding sources of migration and reinvasion.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This form records surrounding sources of migration and reinvasion.

items.text: The following should also be recorded:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The following should also be recorded:

items: Presence of a pile of rejected fruit.
Presence of a completed field that has not been shredded.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Presence of a pile of rejected fruit.
[[prepared]]Presence of a completed field that has not been shredded.

items.text: Assessment of Risk from an Adjacent Source

items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Risk may be classified as:

items: Or a healthy, newly planted tomato field.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Or a healthy, newly planted tomato field.

items: Tomato or potato in production with limited infestation.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Tomato or potato in production with limited infestation.

items: Shredding or plant removal has begun.
A newly planted tomato field lies in the opposite direction.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]A newly planted tomato field lies in the opposite direction.

items.text: To prevent transfer of live pest stages into the field.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]To prevent transfer of live pest stages into the field.
[[prepared]]When:

items: No clear infestation source is present.

items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]When:

items: Limited infestation is confined to separate trays.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Limited infestation is confined to separate trays.

items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]When:

items: Freedom from infestation cannot be guaranteed.
The batch is distributed across more than one area within an infested nursery.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]The batch is distributed across more than one area within an infested nursery.

items.text: Spraying the batch alone should not be accepted as proof that it is safe for transport.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Spraying the batch alone should not be accepted as proof that it is safe for transport.
[[prepared]]Distribution should consider:

items: Separating edge traps from interior traps.
Not changing location without recording it.
Avoiding placement where plants obstruct the trap.
Adjusting height as the crop grows.
Documenting additional traps used during emergencies.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Avoiding placement where plants obstruct the trap.

items.text: Counts from traps with different designs or lures should not be combined into one time series without explanation.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Counts from traps with different designs or lures should not be combined into one time series without explanation.

items.text: Trap condition should be recorded as:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Trap condition should be recorded as:

items.text: Daily catch = Number of males ÷ Number of valid operating days
Days during which the trap was non-functional should not be counted.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Days during which the trap was non-functional should not be counted.
[[prepared]]Also record:

items.text: This record prevents an increase caused by installation of a new lure from being interpreted as a genuine increase in pest abundance.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This record prevents an increase caused by installation of a new lure from being interpreted as a genuine increase in pest abundance.

items.text: Detailed data should not be replaced by one mean alone because distribution may be aggregated.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Detailed data should not be replaced by one mean alone because distribution may be aggregated.
[[prepared]]Calculate:

items.text: To distinguish live eggs expected to hatch from parasitised, hatched, or dead eggs.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]To distinguish live eggs expected to hatch from parasitised, hatched, or dead eggs.

items.text: Parasitism (%) = Number of parasitised eggs ÷ Total evaluable eggs × 100
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Parasitism (%) = Number of parasitised eggs ÷ Total evaluable eggs × 100

items.text: The denominator should be clearly defined.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The denominator should be clearly defined.

items.text: Eggs of undetermined status should not be included in the denominator without explanation.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Eggs of undetermined status should not be included in the denominator without explanation.

items.text: An operational estimate may be used:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]An operational estimate may be used:

items.text: Eggs expected to hatch = Live unparasitised eggs

items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Bearing in mind that:

items: Early parasitism may not yet be visible.
Some eggs may die for other reasons.
The number is an estimate, not an absolute prediction.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Early parasitism may not yet be visible.
[[prepared]]Some eggs may die for other reasons.
[[prepared]]The number is an estimate, not an absolute prediction.

items.text: Evidence of activity should be recorded, such as:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Evidence of activity should be recorded, such as:

items.text: Percentage of a larval instar = Number of larvae in the specified instar ÷ Total live larvae × 100
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Percentage of a [[term:larval-instar]]larval instar[[/term]] = Number of larvae in the specified instar ÷ Total live larvae × 100
[[prepared]]Instar distribution assists in:

items: Analysing failure of Bt or insecticides targeting early instars.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Analysing failure of Bt or insecticides targeting early instars.

items.text: Growing-Point, Stem, and Cluster Inspection Record
Greater weight should be given to growing points and clusters in:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Greater weight should be given to growing points and clusters in:
[[prepared]]The following should be specified:

items.text: Only visibly infested fruits should not be selected; a representative sample should be inspected.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Only visibly infested fruits should not be selected; a representative sample should be inspected.

items.text: Calculation of Fruit Infestation by Number
Fruit infestation by number (%) = Number of infested fruits ÷ Total fruits inspected × 100
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Fruit infestation by number (%) = Number of infested fruits ÷ Total fruits inspected × 100

items.text: Confirmed infestation should be separated from holes of undetermined origin.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Confirmed infestation should be separated from holes of undetermined origin.

rows: Weight of fruits with associated rot
Weight of fruits rejected for other reasons
prepared_rows: [[prepared]]Weight of fruits rejected for other reasons

items.text: Weight loss caused by Tuta (%) = Weight of fruits rejected because of Tuta ÷ Total sample weight × 100
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Weight loss caused by Tuta (%) = Weight of fruits rejected because of Tuta ÷ Total sample weight × 100

items.text: All rotten fruits should not be attributed to Tuta without evidence of an associated wound or infestation.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]All rotten fruits should not be attributed to Tuta without evidence of an associated wound or infestation.

items.text: Nymphs should be recorded separately because their presence indicates:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Nymphs should be recorded separately because their presence indicates:

items: That observations are not limited to migrating or recently released adults.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]That observations are not limited to migrating or recently released adults.

items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Also record:

items: Plant injury caused by mirids where present.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Plant injury caused by mirids where present.

items.text: Not every dead larva should be classified as parasitised.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Not every dead larva should be classified as parasitised.

items.text: A reference sample of cards should be retained to estimate:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A reference sample of cards should be retained to estimate:

items.text: Emergence (%) = Number of parasitoid emergence holes ÷ Number of evaluable parasitised hosts × 100
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Emergence (%) = Number of parasitoid emergence holes ÷ Number of evaluable parasitised hosts × 100
[[prepared]]After release, record:

items.text: Distribution of cards alone is not proof of programme success.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Distribution of cards alone is not proof of programme success.

items.text: Assessment should be conducted at successive intervals and include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Assessment should be conducted at successive intervals and include:

items.text: Predator success should not be assessed after only one day.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Predator success should not be assessed after only one day.

items.text: Movement of beneficial organisms into tomato should be recorded, not only their presence within the belt.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Movement of beneficial organisms into tomato should be recorded, not only their presence within the belt.

items.text: These data are especially important for:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]These data are especially important for:

items.text: Product success should not be measured by mortality alone, but also by feeding cessation, new infestation, and fruit protection.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Product success should not be measured by mortality alone, but also by feeding cessation, new infestation, and fruit protection.
[[prepared]]Also record:

items.text: Switching between two active ingredients in the same group is not genuine rotation.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Switching between two active ingredients in the same group is not genuine rotation.

items.text: Record of All Treatments Affecting Tuta
Applications targeting the following should also be included:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Applications targeting the following should also be included:

items.text: When the material may affect Tuta absoluta or its natural enemies.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]When the material may affect Tuta absoluta or its natural enemies.

items.text: Exposure to the group occurs even when Tuta is not the pest named in the spray order.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Exposure to the group occurs even when Tuta is not the pest named in the spray order.

items.text: This card demonstrates that not spraying was a technical decision based on data.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This card demonstrates that not spraying was a technical decision based on data.

items.text: Nozzles showing unacceptable deviation from the others should be replaced.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Nozzles showing unacceptable deviation from the others should be replaced.

items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Where possible, water-sensitive cards should be placed at:

items.text: A jar test should be recorded when more than one product is used.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A jar test should be recorded when more than one product is used.

items.text: The model name and source of its constants should be recorded and should not be changed during the season without explanation.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The model name and source of its constants should be recorded and should not be changed during the season without explanation.

rows: Contingency plan in case of failure

items.text: The relevant market or standard should be confirmed because limits may differ among markets.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The relevant market or standard should be confirmed because limits may differ among markets.

items.text: Acceptance (%) = Accepted yield weight ÷ Total harvested weight × 100
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Acceptance (%) = Accepted yield weight ÷ Total harvested weight × 100
[[prepared]]Tuta should be separated from:

items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The final cause should be classified as:

items: Failure to select the correct stage.

items.text: The phrase “the insecticide does not work” should not be used without assigning a classification.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The phrase “the insecticide does not work” should not be used without assigning a classification.

rows: Number of times the group was used
prepared_rows: [[prepared]]Number of times the group was used

items.text: The test should be conducted using a standardised protocol and should control the concentration of active ingredient, not merely the commercial product.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The test should be conducted using a standardised protocol and should control the concentration of active ingredient, not merely the commercial product.

items.text: The following codes may be used:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The following codes may be used:

items: T1: Sudden increase in trap catch.
T3: Spread of L1 and L2.

items.text: Coding allows the number of each emergency type to be analysed at the end of the season.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Coding allows the number of each emergency type to be analysed at the end of the season.

items.text: Record of Removal of Infested Fruits and Plants
Infested material should not be left beside the field or along workers' routes.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Infested material should not be left beside the field or along workers' routes.

items: No infested fruits remain on the soil surface.
Monitoring continues for an appropriate period.
Date of complete termination has been recorded.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]No infested fruits remain on the soil surface.
[[prepared]]Date of complete termination has been recorded.

items.text: Assessment of Inspector or Inspection-Team Performance
To verify consistency of measurements among inspectors.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]To verify consistency of measurements among inspectors.

items.text: A joint periodic inspection should be conducted to prevent differences in definitions and counting methods.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A joint periodic inspection should be conducted to prevent differences in definitions and counting methods.
[[prepared]]They should be trained to:

items: Enter zero instead of leaving a cell blank.

items.text: The two meanings should be distinguished.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The two meanings should be distinguished.

items.text: Net benefit = Total benefits − Total costs
Benefit–cost ratio = Total benefits ÷ Total costs
Cost of protecting one accepted tonne = Cost of the Tuta programme ÷ Tonnes of accepted yield
The following questions should be asked:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The following questions should be asked:

items: Was the source internal or external?
What modification is essential for the following season?

rows: First date fruit infestation was detected

items.text: Field names alone should not be used because the same names may recur across seasons.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Field names alone should not be used because the same names may recur across seasons.

items.text: Difference Between Zero and a Blank Cell
Means that inspection was conducted and the indicator was not detected.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Means that inspection was conducted and the indicator was not detected.

items.text: Means that data are unavailable or the inspection was not conducted.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Means that data are unavailable or the inspection was not conducted.

items.text: The system should not automatically convert blank cells to zero because this creates an artificial decline in mean infestation.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The system should not automatically convert blank cells to zero because this creates an artificial decline in mean infestation.

items.text: Fixed lists should be used to reduce data-entry errors.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Fixed lists should be used to reduce data-entry errors.

items.text: The system may generate an alert when:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The system may generate an alert when:

items: A trap reading is entered without operating days.
An application date is later than the harvest date.
The rate falls outside the entered range.
Fruit infestation is entered without the number of fruits inspected.
Number of parasitised eggs exceeds the total.
A treatment has no reassessment date.
A terminated field lacks a removal record.

[[prepared]]An application date is later than the harvest date.
[[prepared]]The rate falls outside the entered range.
[[prepared]]Fruit infestation is entered without the number of fruits inspected.
[[prepared]]Number of parasitised eggs exceeds the total.
[[prepared]]A terminated field lacks a removal record.

items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The system should:

items: Retain an audit trail of changes.
Prevent deletion of original records without documentation.
Save photographs and reports using field codes.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Prevent deletion of original records without documentation.
[[prepared]]Save photographs and reports using field codes.

items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]These procedures are important when data are used for:
[[prepared]]The dashboard may display:

items.text: The dashboard should not issue an automatic spray order without technical review.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The dashboard should not issue an automatic spray order without technical review.

items.text: Records should be reviewed weekly to confirm:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Records should be reviewed weekly to confirm:

items: Presence of a signature or responsible person's name.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Presence of a signature or responsible person's name.

items.text: The supervisor should select a sample of records and repeat:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The supervisor should select a sample of records and repeat:

items: Inspection of a number of plants.

items.text: Actual conditions should be compared with the recorded information.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Actual conditions should be compared with the recorded information.
[[prepared]]These include:

items: Percentage of rounds recorded on time.
Percentage of traps with valid data.
Percentage of treatments that were reassessed.
Percentage of samples with a complete chain of custody.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Percentage of samples with a complete chain of custody.

items.text: Records should be retained for a period sufficient to allow:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Records should be retained for a period sufficient to allow:
[[prepared]]The following should be retained:

items.text: Retention should comply with project procedures and regulatory and commercial requirements.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Retention should comply with project procedures and regulatory and commercial requirements.

items.text: Error One: Recording Only the Mean
This conceals hotspots and variation among plants.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This conceals hotspots and variation among plants.

items.text: Error Two: Failing to Code Fields and Sectors
This causes data to be mixed.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This causes data to be mixed.

items.text: Error Three: Leaving Cells Blank When the Pest Is Absent
It becomes impossible to know whether inspection was conducted.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]It becomes impossible to know whether inspection was conducted.

items.text: Error Four: Recording the Product Name Without the Active Ingredient

items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Mode-of-action analysis becomes difficult.

items.text: Error Five: Failing to Record the IRAC Group

items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Resistance management fails.

items.text: Error Six: Failing to Record Other Target Pests
Tuta may be exposed indirectly to the same group.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Tuta may be exposed indirectly to the same group.

items.text: Error Seven: Failing to Record Weather
Application failure becomes difficult to interpret.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Application failure becomes difficult to interpret.

items.text: Error Eight: Failing to Record Trap Condition
Data from a non-functional trap may be used.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Data from a non-functional trap may be used.

items.text: Error Nine: Failing to Separate Edges from the Interior

items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Evidence of immigration is lost.

items.text: Error Ten: Counting Old Mines as Current Activity

items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The population is artificially inflated.

items.text: Error Eleven: Failing to Separate Reasons for Fruit Rejection
Other losses are attributed to Tuta.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Other losses are attributed to Tuta.

items.text: Error Twelve: Recording a Release Without Evaluating Emergence
This does not prove biological-agent quality.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This does not prove biological-agent quality.

items.text: Error Thirteen: Implementing a Treatment Without a Documented Order
The reason for treatment becomes unclear.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The reason for treatment becomes unclear.

items.text: Error Fourteen: Failing to Document a Non-Spray Decision
A technically correct decision may appear to be neglect.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A technically correct decision may appear to be neglect.

items.text: Error Fifteen: Modifying Original Data Without an Audit Trail

items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The system loses credibility.

items.text: Minimum Requirements for a Large Commercial Project

items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]These should include:

items: Chain of custody for resistance samples.
1. Adopt one standard code for each field.
2. Standardise the unit used for trap readings.
3. Separate field edges from the interior.
7. Measure fruit infestation by number and weight.
13. Maintain resistance samples under a chain of custody.
15. Measure days between final harvest and field termination.
16. Create a database for factories and projects.
19. Analyse results at the end of the season.
20. Use the data to develop local thresholds.

items.text: A sound database converts individual experience into institutional knowledge that can be used to develop more efficient programmes, local economic thresholds, and an Egyptian early-warning system.

items.text: CABI and Koppert Biological Systems. Integrated Management of Tomato Leaf Miner and Other Tomato Pests: A Training Manual for Extension Workers.

Insecticide Resistance Action Committee. Tuta absoluta Insecticide Resistance Management Guidelines.
Insecticide Resistance Action Committee. IRAC Mode of Action Classification Scheme.
Insecticide Resistance Action Committee. Susceptibility Test Method No. 022: Tuta absoluta Larvae.

Guedes, R. N. C., Roditakis, E., Campos, M. R., et al. (2019). Insecticide resistance in the tomato pinworm Tuta absoluta: Patterns, spread, mechanisms, management and outlook. Journal of Pest Science, 92, 1329–1342.
